title: acer ginnala subsp. euginnala
intro: Acer ginnala subsp. euginnala, also known as the Amur maple or Korean maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain, inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Acer ginnala subsp. euginnala include reducing fever, relieving headache and cough, as well as supporting immune system function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
